channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Why Humans Still Burn Logs for Power, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Indigenous Lands & Innovation Topics include: renewable energy, solar, data sovereignty, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Why Humans Still Burn Logs for Power
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] ‘Carney is at a crossroads’ with First Nations,...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] Watch: how First Nations are leading the clean...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Negotiations over proposed South Oka...

Additional sources provided:

  • CNET: [CNET] I Review Routers for a Living. Don’t Buy a Router Rig...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Just 14 Species Have ‘Genuinely’ Improved on BC’s...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Tumbler Ridge Shows We Can’t Take Rural Medicine ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake council drops controve...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Testing Severely Neglected VHS Tapes and CDs
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I tested Meta AI vs Deepseek with 5 real-world ...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] US lawmakers push for pause in data cen...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Demonstrating Gray Codes With Industrial Display
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Oldest Dog DNA Ever Found Reveals How Ancient...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] This discounted Wi-Fi 7 mesh system has me seriously...
  • The Marginalian: [The Marginalian] Gardening as Resistance: Notes on Building...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Skip Synology and grab this underrated NAS ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Redevelopment project at Jubilee Place in Williams Lake adjusted following provincial budget and [100 Mile Free Press] Air quality monitors to be installed at Watch Lake VFD, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Community Tech & Governance Topics include: broadband, renewable energy, data sovereignty, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Redevelopment project at Jubilee Pla...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Air quality monitors to be installed a...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile House ER to be closed for six...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Realtor nets $20K fine for false adv...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] BC Cuts Climate Agency, Sends Some Staff to Work ...

Additional sources provided:

  • WIRED: [WIRED] What You Need to Know About the Foreign-Made Router ...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] BC’s Auditor General Offers Reasons for the Lack ...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] AmneziaVPN launches AmneziaWG 2.0 – and it's a ...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] This is the best Home Assistant project you ca...
  • CNET: [CNET] Best VPN for Mac for 2026: Improve Your Privacy for W...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Inside BC’s Plan to Fast-Track Environmental Asse...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Snow warning for Highway 1, wind sta...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] 3D Printed Robot Arm Built For Learning Purposes
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I tested Grok vs. Claude with the same 5 prompt...
  • NYT Well: [NYT Well] Why a Strong Butt Is Key to Healthy Aging
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] I built an app for work in 5 minutes with Tasklet
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Testing Expensive Graphene-Reinforced Nylon Filam...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Unpaid Subcontractors for Recovery Centres Say UC...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. government won’t reveal DRIPA c...
  • MacRumors: [MacRumors] Nuki Keypad 2 NFC Brings Aliro-Powered Tap-to-Un...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Unfilled and [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Fire Department responds to vehicle fire in Dairy Fields, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Working Lands & Industry Topics include: satellite, connectivity, precision agriculture, solar, AI.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Un...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Fire Department respon...

  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Sunken Soviet Submarine Is Quietly Leaking Radiati...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Hoover Dam could lose most of its power as Lak...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C., UBC partnering on $40M fund to...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I built a gesture-controlled desk buddy wit...

  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Harvesting Burned Trees May Seem a No-Brainer. Bu...

  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Undercover officer testifies at gangst...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] Opera GX for Linux is way more than great gaming bro...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] The 'Biggest Mistake' People Make With GLP-1 ...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] 60-sec cube camper rises & slides into much more...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Stop wasting 10% of your SSD on an old tric...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] PicoZ80 is a Drop-in Replacement for Everyone’s F...
  • MacRumors: [MacRumors] Kwikset Smart Locks Can Now Be Controlled Throug...

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Google unveils new ‘vibe design’ tool to help anyone design a high-fidelity UI using natural language and [Business Insider] This 16-year-old refused a $300,000 offer to drop out of high school and now runs his own AI company, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling Topics include: broadband, AI, infrastructure, funding, pilot project.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Google unveils new ‘vibe design’ tool to help an...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] This 16-year-old refused a $300,000 offer...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Microsoft just killed triangle-by-triangle ray...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Comox Hells Angels trial faces stay ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Lions Club hosts Clubs...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] PHOTOS: Sidney dive reveals years...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] As a nonprofit-focused software company, ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Schools are putting AI in classrooms before they kn...

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Three high-risk AI vulnerabilities discovered in...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Iain Black endorsed for Conservative...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Andrej Karpathy says he's using his Jense...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Uber and Rivian are teaming up for a big robotaxi p...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Building a Laser-Driven Photoacoustic Speaker
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Homelab projects to try this weekend (March 20
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Building an LC Meter with a Franklin Oscillator
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I gave my favorite pocketable mini PC an instan...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] U.N. Climate Report Finds Growing Energy I...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Magnetic Fluid Injected Into The Heart May Pr...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Off-grid micro house fits everything you need in...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I built a $15 ESP32 display that lets me mo...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Tech Employees Are Reportedly Being Evaluated by H...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Why stolen credentials continue to work even whe...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] NymVPN's latest update brings crucial anti-censo...

item.title: Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Kottke.org] Battery prices continue to fall . “Lithium-ion... and [100 Mile Free Press] Gateway water system under boil water advisory, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Resilient Rural Futures Topics include: broadband, satellite, connectivity, renewable energy, solar.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. allowing old-growth logging despite protection promise, experts say and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prince George-North Cariboo MLA introduces new bill to support victims of car accidents, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Cariboo Voices & Local News Topics include: broadband, data sovereignty, AI, digital divide, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Fishing photos needed for 2026 Gateway fishing guide and [ScienceAlert] Something Else Used to Drive Climate Changes, Ancient Ice Cores Reveal, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life Topics include: satellite, AI, infrastructure, maintenance, funding.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Only 9% of global firms are ready to handle AI-driven threats, and many are dangerously overconfident and [TechCrunch] Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Indigenous Lands & Innovation Topics include: broadband, fiber, connectivity, telemedicine, renewable energy.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Trans Mountain begins assessment work in North Thompson and [Lifehacker] You Can Turn an Old Android Phone Into a Wifi Extender for Your Home Network, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] 2,400 apply for 200-250 firefighter jobs as B.C. gears up for wildfire season and [Nautilus] Babies Learn the Art of Deception Before Their First Birthday, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.
